sanskrit:
साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदुः।
प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतसः॥
transliteration: sādhibhūtādhidaivaṁ māṁ sādhiyajñaṁ ca ye viduḥ / prayāṇa-kāle ’pi ca māṁ te vidur yukta-cetasaḥ
literal_meaning: Those who know Me as the principle underlying all beings, all gods, and all sacrifice—even at the moment of death—know Me with a steadfast mind.
contextual_meaning: A person who has internalized a single, coherent framework of meaning (Krishna) can face mortality without cognitive fragmentation or terror; the psyche remains integrated because the object of attention is no longer the transient self but the enduring pattern that holds every subsystem together.
psychological_state: Post-integrative acceptance: the ego no longer needs to defend against death because it has located its identity in a trans-personal constant; anxiety dissolves into steady, panoramic awareness.
trigger_scenario: Receiving a terminal diagnosis and, instead of panic, experiencing a calm certainty that every relationship, ritual, and bit of matter around you is held within the same luminous field you have trusted all your life.
anchor_text: Hold the thread that weaves all beings and meet death unshaken.
